trigger_reason: The agent prioritizes high-level summarization (MITRE mapping/timelines) over mandatory granular extraction, treating documentation of negative results and technical IOCs as optional rather than foundational requirements.
improvements: Enforce a 'Non-Empty Hash' validation: Any binary/script identified must return a valid SHA-256; if null, the system must trigger a secondary search block.
Formalize 'Proof of Absence' validation: Require citation of specific log sources (e.g., Event ID 4624, specific Registry hives) for every negative claim to be considered 'documented'.
Require an 'Evidence Log' footer: Every investigation must include a manifest of all tools and log sources queried during the forensic triage.

checklist.note: Reconstruct sequence with precise timestamps; cite log sources (e.g., Sysmon Event ID 1) for every jump in the timeline.
Mandatory SHA-256 hashes for all binaries. Null placeholders are strictly forbidden; system must report 'Not Found' with search query details if hash unavailable.
Map to MITRE ATT&CK sub-techniques; provide specific process arguments or log evidence for each identified tactic.
Report findings or specify the memory scan range/tool used. If not applicable, explicitly state the justification based on the alert scope.
For 'No movement' claims: Must cite specific log source/Event IDs (e.g., 4624/4648/SMB logs) analyzed to reach this conclusion.
Document specific Registry keys or Scheduled Task names checked. 'No persistence found' is an invalid claim without citing the paths queried.
Assess volume/destination and link directly to Netflow or Firewall log evidence identifiers.
